name: orsopy

metadata.name: orsopy
metadata.summary: Support library of the Open Reflectometry Standards Organization (ORSO)

documentation:
# orsopy

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/orsopy.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/orsopy
```

```{image} https://github.com/reflectivity/orsopy/actions/workflows/pytest.yml/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/reflectivity/orsopy/actions/workflows/pytest.yml
```

```{image} https://github.com/reflectivity/orsopy/actions/workflows/docs_build.yml/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/reflectivity/orsopy/actions/workflows/docs_build.yml
```

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/github/reflectivity/orsopy/badge.svg?branch=main
:alt: Coverage Level
:target: https://coveralls.io/github/reflectivity/orsopy?branch=main
```

orsopy is a Python library that implements ORSO functionality, which currently includes the [reduced data file format].
The orsopy package is used by a range of data reduction and analysis packages for the writing and reading of reduced reflectometry data.
This data is written following the [ORSO defined specification], enabling a metadata-rich and flexible file to be created.

[ORSO] is an open organisation aimed at improving the scientific techniques of neutron and X-ray reflectometry.
In the interest of transparency, all minutes from orsopy developer meetings are available in the [Documents] in the sidebar of this page.
If you are interested in getting involved in developing orsopy, please feel free to [contribute] or get in touch on the [ORSO Slack] (where there is a channel dedicated to orsopy).

## Features

- [Reading and writing of ORSO specification reduced reflectivity files].

[contribute]: https://www.reflectometry.org/orsopy/contributing
[documents]: https://www.reflectometry.org/orsopy/documents
[orso]: https://www.reflectometry.org
[orso defined specification]: https://www.reflectometry.org/file_format/specification
[orso slack]: https://join.slack.com/t/orso-co/shared_invite/zt-z7p3v89g-~JgCbzcxurQP6ufqdfTCfw
[reading and writing of orso specification reduced reflectivity files]: modules.html#fileio
[reduced data file format]: https://www.reflectometry.org/file_formats/

documentation:
A description of the reduction that has been performed.

Software used for reduction.

param timestamp

Datetime of reduced file creation.

classes.name: orsopy.fileio.data_source.Experiment
documentation:
A definition of the experiment performed.

```
param title

Proposal or project title.

param instrument

Reflectometer identifier.

param start_date

Start date for the experiment.

param probe

Radiation probe, either  or
.

param facility

Facility where the experiment was performed.

param proposalID

Identifier for experiment at a facility.

param doi

Digital object identifier for the experiment, possibly
provided by the facility.
```

classes.name: orsopy.fileio.data_source.Sample
documentation:
A description of the sample measured.

```
param name

An identified for the individual sample or the subject and
state being measured.

param category

Simple sample description, front (beam side) / back,
each side should be one of ,
, ,
, , .

param composition

Notes on the nominal composition of the sample e.g.
.

param description

Further details of the sample, e.g. size.

param size

Sample size in x, y, z direction, where z is parallel to the surface normal
and x is along the beam direction (important for footprint correction).

param environment

Name of the sample environment device(s).

param sample_parameters

Dictionary of sample parameters. See Sample.PARAMETER_DEFAULT_KEYS
for some common names defined in the specification.

param model

A model using the simple model description  (https://www.reflectometry.org/projects/simple_model)
```

classes.name: orsopy.fileio.data_source.Polarization

class_variables.name: orsopy.fileio.data_source.Polarization.pi
documentation: in scattering plane

class_variables.name: orsopy.fileio.data_source.Polarization.sigma
documentation: perpendicular to scattering plane

class_variables.name: orsopy.fileio.data_source.Polarization.left
documentation: circular left

class_variables.name: orsopy.fileio.data_source.Polarization.right
documentation: circular right

Polarization of the beam used for the reflectivity.

Neutrons:
The first symbol indicates the magnetisation direction of the incident
beam, the second symbol indicates the direction of the scattered
beam. If either polarization or analysis are not employed the
symbol is replaced by "o".

X-rays:
Uses the conventional names pi, sigma, left and right. In experiments
with polarization analysis the incident and outgoing polarizations
are separated with an underscore "_".

```

modules.name: orsopy.fileio.orso
documentation: Implementation of the top level class for the ORSO header.

functions.name: orsopy.fileio.orso.save_orso
documentation:
Saves an ORSO file. Each of the datasets must have a unique
{py:attr}`OrsoDataset.info.data_set` attribute. If that attribute is not
set, it is given an integer value corresponding to it's position
in the list.

```
param datasets

List of OrsoDataset to save into the Orso file.

param fname

The file name to save to.

param comment

Comment to write at the top of Orso file.

param data_separator

Optional string of newline characters to separate multiple datasets.

raises ValueError

If the 
values are not unique.
```

functions.name: orsopy.fileio.orso.load_orso
documentation:
---
param fname: The Orso file to load.
return: |-
  {py:class}`OrsoDataset` objects for each dataset contained
  within the ORT file.
---

```

methods.name: orsopy.fileio.orso.Orso.to_dict
documentation: Adds the user data to the returned dictionary.

The Orso object collects the necessary metadata.

```
param data_source

Information about the origin and ownership of
the raw data.

param reduction

Details of the data reduction that has been
performed. The content of this section should contain enough
information to rerun the reduction.

param columns

Information about the columns of data that will
be contained in the file.

param data_set

An identifier for the data set, i.e. if there is
more than one data set in the object.
```

documentation:
Save the {py:class}`OrsoDataset`.

```
param fname

The file name to save to.
```

---
param data: |-
  The numerical data associated with the reflectivity
  measurement. The data has shape {code}`(npnts, ncols)`.
param info: The header information for the reflectivity measurement
raises ValueError: When {code}`ncols != len(self.info.columns)`.
---

modules.name: orsopy.fileio.model_language
documentation:
Implementation of the simplified model language for the ORSO header.

It includes parsing of models from header or different input information and
resolving the model to a simple list of slabs.

modules.name: orsopy.slddb.element_table.nabs_geant4
documentation: Neutron cross sections extracted from Geant4 by the ESS dgcode framework (doi:10.1016/j.physb.2018.03.025).

modules.name: orsopy.slddb.element_table
documentation:
Package containing all elemental data used for SLD calculations.

Main function is get_element(value) that returns an Element object for a given Z-number or identifier.
The objects are stored in a dictionary for faster lookup as well as keeping the objects for each element the same.

modules.name: orsopy.fileio.base
documentation: Implementation of the base classes for the ORSO header.

functions.name: orsopy.fileio.base._read_header_data
documentation:
Reads the header and data contained within an ORSO file, parsing it into
json dictionaries and numerical arrays.

# Parameters

```
param file

File to read.

param validate

Validates the file against the ORSO json schema.
Requires that the jsonschema package be installed.

return

The tuple contains:
- First item is a list of json dicts containing the parsed yaml
header. This has to be processed further.
- Second item is a Python list containing numpy arrays holding the
reflectometry data in the file. It's contained in a list because
each of the datasets may have a different number of columns.
- Final item is the ORSO version string.
```

functions.name: orsopy.fileio.base._validate_header_data
documentation:
Checks whether a json dictionary corresponds to a valid ORSO header.

Obtain these dct_list by loading from \_read_header_data first.

```
param dct_list

Dicts corresponding to parsed yaml headers from the ORT file.

raises ValueError

When the first four columns are not named correctly
(i.e. , , , ).

raises ValueError

When the units for the  column is not
.

raises ValueError

When the units for columns  and
 are not the same.
```

functions.name: orsopy.fileio.base._possibly_open_file
documentation:
Context manager for files.

```
param f

If  is a file, then yield the file. If  is a str then
open the file and yield the newly opened file. On leaving this
context manager the file is closed, if it was opened by this
context manager (i.e.  was a string).

param modes

An optional string that specifies the mode in which
the file is opened.

yields

On leaving the context manager the file is closed, if it
was opened by this context manager.
```

functions.name: orsopy.fileio.base._todict

Recursively converts an object to a dict representation
<https://stackoverflow.com/questions/1036409>
Licenced under CC BY-SA 4.0

```
param obj

Object to convert to dict representation.

param classkey

Key to be assigned to class objects.

return

Dictionary representation of object.
```

functions.name: orsopy.fileio.base._nested_update
documentation:
Nested dictionary update.

For each subclass of Header, collect optional arguments in

methods.name: orsopy.fileio.base.Header._resolve_type
documentation:
This function (recursively for {py:class}`Union` and
\:py:class\`Optional\` objects) populates different attributes,
including constructing {py:class}`Header` object. Once the given
object is created it is returned. If it is not possible to
determine the correct object type {code}`None` is returned.

```
param hint

The type of the given field.

param item

An unresolved item to populate attribute.

return

Correctly resolved object with required type for orso
compatibility.
```

classes.name: orsopy.fileio.base.ErrorValue

methods.name: orsopy.fileio.base.ErrorValue.sigma
documentation:
Return value converted to standard deviation.

The conversion factors can be found in common statistics and experimental physics text books or derived
manually solving the variance definition integral.
(e.g. Dekking, Michel (2005).
A modern introduction to probability and statistics : understanding why and how.
Springer, London, UK:)
Values and some references available on Wikipedia, too.
Information about errors on a value.

classes.name: orsopy.fileio.base.ValueVector

methods.name: orsopy.fileio.base.ValueVector.as_unit
documentation: Returns a (x, y, z) tuple of values as converted to the given unit.

A vector or list of vectors with an optional unit.
For vectors relating to the sample, such as polarisation,
the follow definitions are used.

```
param x

is defined as parallel to the radiation beam, positive going
with the beam direction.

param y

is defined from the other two based on the right hand rule.

param z

is defined as normal to the sample surface, positive direction
in scattering direction.

param unit

SI unit string.
```

documentation:
Rauch, H. and Waschkowski, W. (2003) Neutron Scattering Lengths in ILL Neutron Data Booklet (second edition),
A.-J. Dianoux, G. Lander, Eds. Old City Publishing, Philidelphia, PA. pp 1.1-1 to 1.1-17.

modules.name: orsopy.slddb.element_table.nlengths
documentation:
https://www.ncnr.nist.gov/resources/n-lengths/list.html
Neutron scattering lengths and cross sections
Isotope conc    Coh b   Inc b   Coh xs  Inc xs  Scatt xs        Abs xs

modules.name: orsopy.slddb.element_table.masses
documentation:
Raw data imported from NIST https://www.nist.gov/pml/atomic-weights-and-isotopic-compositions-relative-atomic-masses
The atomic weights data were published by J. Meija et al in Atomic Weights of the Elements 2013, 
and the isotopic compositions data were published by M. Berglund and
M.E. Wieser in Isotopic Compositions of the Elements 2009.
The relative atomic masses of the isotopes data were published by M. Wang, G. Audi, A.H. Wapstra, F.G. Kondev,
M. MacCormick, X. Xu1, and B. Pfeiffer in The AME2012 Atomic Mass Evaluation.

modules.name: orsopy.slddb.constants
documentation:
Physical constants and conversion factors used in the package.

The values given here have been taken from the
CODATA Internationally recommended 2018 values of the Fundamental Physical Constants
provided at https://physics.nist.gov/cuu/Constants/index.html

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.u2g
documentation: 1/N_Avogadro (g/mol => g/atom)

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.h_eVs
documentation: eV·s Planck's constant

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.h_Js
documentation: J·s Planck's constant

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.m_n
documentation: kg neutron mass

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.c_ms
documentation: m/s speed of light

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.r_e
documentation: fm - classical electron radius

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.r_e_angstrom
documentation: Å - classical electron radius

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.sigma_to_b_1A
documentation: fm/barn absorption at 1.0Å (sigma=4*pi*b/k)

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.wavelength_for_b
documentation: 1.798 Å (wavelength at 2200 m/s, the default for reported b-values)

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.sigma_to_b
documentation: fm/barn absorption at 1.798Å (sigma=4*pi*b/k)

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.E_to_lambda
documentation: keV·Å conversion x-ray energy to wavelength (h*c)

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.muB
documentation: kA/m Å³ - Bohr Magneton scaled to get kA/m from Ä³ FU volume

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.Cu_kalpha1
documentation: keV

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.Cu_kalpha2
documentation: keV

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.Cu_kalpha
documentation: keV

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.Mo_kalpha1
documentation: keV

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.Mo_kalpha2
documentation: keV

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.Mo_kalpha
documentation: keV

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.rho_of_M
documentation: Å^-2 from kA/m

variables.name: orsopy.slddb.constants.dens_H2O
documentation: Density of heavy water, TSING-LIEN CHANG & LÜ-HO TUNG, Nature 163, page737 (1949)

modules.name: orsopy.slddb.material

Class to hold information for one material and allow calculation
of x-ray and neutron SLDs for different applications.

documentation:
Used to represent a chain of amino acids. Currently, no checking
is done and this is just a string containing the appropriate letters.

classes.name: orsopy.slddb.material.Material

methods.name: orsopy.slddb.material.Material.deuterate
documentation: Return a partially deuterated molecule with fraction of D instead of H.

methods.name: orsopy.slddb.material.Material.exchange
documentation: Return a partially deuterated modlecule within H2O/D2O solution given amount of exchange.

methods.name: orsopy.slddb.material.Material.export
documentation:
Export material data to dictionary.

xray_units: one of "edens", "n_db" or "sld"

methods.name: orsopy.slddb.material.Material.__mul__
documentation:
Calculate a multiple of this material, mostly useful when combining
different components. The SLD should stay the same as the formula
as well as the FU_volume are multiplied with the same amount.

Units used:
b: fm
fu_volume: Å³
fu_dens: 1/Å³
dens: g/cm³
roh_n: Å^{-2}
roh_m: Å^{-2}
mu: muB/FU
M: kA/m = emu/cm³

modules.name: orsopy.slddb
documentation:
Package for a database of Scattering Length Density data (SLD) for neutron
and x-ray scattering.
The query to the DB includes recalculation of SLD for needed radiation.

Part of ORSO initiative, see: https://www.reflectometry.org/

Contributers:
    Artur Glavic <artur.glavic@psi.ch>

methods.name: orsopy.slddb.comparators.Comparator.query_args

Return the arguments used for the query. The number of arguments should

classes.name: orsopy.slddb.converters.CMail
documentation: The user email gets encrypted, which does not allow verification on database level
class CMail(ValidatedString):
regex = re.compile(r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b', re.IGNORECASE)
placeholder = 'your.name@domain.net'
def html_input(self, field, value):
return f'<input type="email" name="{field}" id="compound {field}" value="{value}"' \
' placeholder="'+self.placeholder+'" title="'+self.placeholder+'" />'

methods.name: orsopy.slddb.converters.CLimited.__init__
documentation: a converter for numbers that checks the range

classes.name: orsopy.slddb.webapi.SLD_API

methods.name: orsopy.slddb.webapi.SLD_API.search
documentation:
Search for a particular material using a combination of provided search keys.

Examples:
     api.search(formula="Fe2O3")
     api.search(density=5.242)
     api.search(name='iron')

methods.name: orsopy.slddb.webapi.SLD_API.material
documentation:
Returns the material object for a certain database entry specified by its unique ID.

Example:
    res=api.search(formula='Fe')
    material=api.material(res[0]['ID'])
    print(material.dens, material.rho_n, material.f_of_E(8.0))

methods.name: orsopy.slddb.webapi.SLD_API.custom
documentation:
Returns the material object for a certain material as specified by caller.

Example:
    res=api.custom('Fe', dens=7.8)
    print(material.dens, material.rho_n, material.f_of_E(8.0))

methods.name: orsopy.slddb.webapi.SLD_API.bio_blender
documentation: Get material for protein, DNA or RNA. Provide a letter sequence and molecule type ('protein', 'dna', 'rna').

Python API for users of the SLDDB data.

Allows to query the online database for materials, calculate SLDs and add new materials.
If connection to the server fails, a local copy of the database is used, instead.

Usage:
  from orsopy.slddb import api
  res=api.search(formula="Fe2O3")
  res[0]['density'] => ....

  m=api.material(res[0]['ID']) # retrieve all data for the given material, see Material class.
  sldn=m.rho_n # get nuclear neutron SLD (complex number)
  sldm=m.rho_m # get magnetic neutron SLD (real number)
  sldx=m.f_of_E(E=8.047823) # get x-ray SLD (complex number) for given energy, default is Cu-Kalpha

  # custom material just for SLD calculation, requires either dens, fu_volume, rho_n or xsld+xE
  m=api.custom(formula='Au', dens=19.3)

Units of results/queries:
  density: g/cm³
  roh_n: Å^{-2}
  roh_m: Å^{-2}
  sldx: Å^{-2}
  fu_volume: Å³

methods.name: orsopy.utils.density_resolver.DensityResolver.resolve_formula

The returned value is the number density in 1/nm³.

documentation:
Estimates the density of a material from its individual elements
by averaging the bulk element densities.

The returned value is the number density in 1/nm³.
